event: found to have a segment-V space-occupying lesion in the liver
computed tomography scan found lesions in liver segment V had enlarged
Serological tests for hepatitis A negative
Serological tests for hepatitis C negative
donor was a 21-year-old male who suffered brain death from a car accident
intravenous administration of hepatitis B immunoglobulin
received immunosuppressive drugs including steroids and tacrolimus
pathological analyses confirmed HCC with massive tumor necrosis
bleeding in the abdominal cavity ceased
sputum culture suggested methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus
rash advanced into erythematous macules and papules
spread to limbs, palms, neck, and face
oral ulcers on buccal mucosa and lips
skin biopsy performed on left chest
bone marrow pathology no special lesions
skin biopsy consistent with grade-1 acute lt-GVHD
esophageal and oral ulcers continued to worsen
death from septic shock and multiple organ dysfunction
